title: CONTRIBUTION OF MOLECULAR BIOLOGY TO THE ETIOLOGICAL DIAGNOSIS OF INFECTIOUS MENINGITIS
author: MOHAMMED TAHA MEGHRAOUI
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Objectives:  Our study aims to assess the contribution of the Filmarray Meningitis/Encephalitis panel in the diagnosis and management of infectious meningitis by comparing it to conventional culture, while highlighting the epidemiology of the pathogens involved. 

  Methods:  This is a retrospective study that was carried out on 115 CSF samples from patients clinically suspected of meningitis and analyzed by conventional methods and by the Filmarray M / E panel test. This work was carried out over a period of 36 months from January 2018 toDecember2020 at the microbiology laboratory of the Avicenne Military Hospital in Marrakech. 

  Results:  115 samples were documented. The filmarray M/E panel detected at least one pathogen in 26 samples, representing a positivity rate of 22.6%. The infectious agents implicated in meningitis were predominated by viruses (52%, n=14), followed by bacteria (44%, n=11); yeasts are clearly in the minority making 4% (n=1) of the positive samples. The cultures were positive in 6 cases.  Streptococcus pneumoniae  was the most involved microbial agent, constituting a rate of 50% (n=3) of the bacteria detected in the cultures. 

 The concordance rate was 75% for the detection of  Streptococcus pneumoniae . In this study, only one sample was positive on the filmarray Meningitis / Encephalitis panel and negative on culture. 

  Conclusion:  The Syndromic Meningitis / Encephalitis FilmArray-type multiplex PCR is a simple, rapid and reliable diagnostic tool allowing the implementation of an early and adapted therapeutic strategy. However, given the importance of susceptibility testing, the use of molecular tests should be carried out in addition to conventional microbiology.
